title: Youth mental health: About the Youth Mental Health Fund - Canada.ca
content:
## Youth mental health: About the Youth Mental Health Fund
- About the Youth Mental Health Fund
- Integrated Youth Services
Improving access to mental health care for young people across Canada
- About the Youth Mental Health Fund
- Enhancing community-based health services

## About the Youth Mental Health Fund
Young people across Canada are struggling with their mental health. It's crucial that they have timely access to the right services and supports within their communities.
Through the Youth Mental Health Fund, the Government of Canada is making strategic investments to create lasting and meaningful improvements in the mental health of youth and for their families.
This includes investments to:
- Increase access to community-based mental health services
- improve navigation and referral to mental health services and supports
- improve the mental health of youth aged 12 to 25 (with flexibility)
- equip community-based organizations to offer more mental health services tailored to youth and their families and make referrals to other service providers when needed
- provide youth with the mental health supports they need, ensuring a healthy transition into adulthood and the opportunity to reach their full potential
To help make sure that youth mental health services are equitable, culturally safe, accessible and in line with the needs of diverse communities, the fund was designed with input from:
- youth, including equity-deserving youth and Indigenous youth
- health professionals
- national Indigenous organizations
- organizations that serve young people

## Enhancing community-based health services
Reducing obstacles in accessing mental health care is particularly important for youth experiencing health inequities, such as:
- young women and girls
- youth who identify as 2SLGBTQI+
- youth living in low-income households
- refugee, newcomer or immigrant youth
- youth residing in rural and remote areas
- youth experiencing systemic discrimination and racism
- youth facing other social or structural barriers or youth with overlapping realities
Community organizations are key partners in providing youth with the mental health services they need where they live, go to school and spend time with their peers.
By being based in the communities they serve, these organizations gain a deeper understanding of the needs, cultural sensitivities, and challenges local youth face. This helps them provide more effective care.
The fund also supports the development and expansion of Indigenous-led youth mental wellness services shaped by Indigenous youth, communities and partners.
Data on youth mental heath in Canada
- Many mental health conditions first appear in adolescence. Almost two thirds of mental health disorders emerge before the age of 25, and nearly half do before 18. (Mental Health and Access to Care Survey 2022, Health Canada analysis).
- In 2022, 1 in 4 youth in Canada had been diagnosed with a mental illness (Mental Health and Access to Care Survey 2022, Health Canada analysis).
- Suicide is the second leading cause of death among those aged 15 to 34. The suicide rate among Indigenous youth aged 15-24 is 5 to 6 times the rates seen in the non-Indigenous population. (Public Health Agency of Canada).
Agreements have been put in place under the Youth Mental Health Fund. They are listed below. Further investments will be announced over the coming months.

### The Integrated Youth Services Collaboration Centre (IYS CC)
- Organization : Centre for Addiction and Mental Health
- Description : Serve as the pan-Canadian convener for IYS Networks and leaders. Lead collaboration, training and workforce development, data integration and knowledge sharing to support the measurement-based spread of the model across Canada grounded in evidence and youth experience.
- Duration : December 2024 to March 2029
- Project stream : Stream 1
- Contribution agreement : Approximately $16 million

### Pathways to Wellness: Expanding Culturally Safe  Mental Health Care for Youth Across New Brunswick
Organization : Canadian  Mental Health Association – New Brunswick
Location : New Brunswick
Description : As part of expanding its reach  to youth, this initiative will pilot mobile mental health  outreach services in two rural/remote areas of New  Brunswick, design new culturally  safe service pathways into Integrated Youth  Services hubs for high needs youth, including those  experiencing neurodevelopmental or eating disorders,  and will implement virtual services across all new and existing  hubs.
Duration : September  16, 2025 – March 31, 2029
Project stream : Stream 1
Contribution agreement : Approximately $10 million

### Here for Youth: Increasing access and navigation to Integrated Youth Services across Manitoba
- Organization : The United Way of Winnipeg
- Description : Increase access and navigation to Integrated Youth Services across Manitoba. Building on existing successes to open 2 additional Huddle youth hubs. Enhance and expand direct services for youth across Manitoba.
- Duration : February 2025 to March 2029
- Project stream : Stream 1
- Contribution agreement : Approximately $10 million

### Enhancing Health Equity in Integrated Youth Services
- Organization : The Centre for Addiction and Mental Health
- Description : Community integration with specialized, culturally competent care. Strengthen and expand Integrated Youth Services for equity-deserving youth across Ontario. Enhance collaboration between Youth Wellness Hubs Ontario Hub Networks and community organizations.
- Duration : February 2025 to March 2029
- Project stream : Stream 1
- Contribution agreement : Approximately $10 million

### Accelerate access to Integrated Youth Services across British Columbia through the Foundry network
- Organization : Providence Health Care
- Location : British Columbia
- Description : Support the expansion of hubs to more communities and youth who lack physical access to care for equity-deserving populations, including Black, Indigenous and People of Colour, sexually-diverse and gender-diverse youth.
- Duration : February 2025 to March 2029
- Project stream : Stream 1
- Contribution agreement : Approximately $10 million

### Improving Vulnerable Youth Outcomes through a Clinical and Wrap-Around Supports Model of Care
- Organization : Choices for Youth
- Location : Newfoundland and Labrador
- Description : Expanding Family and Natural Supports programs to support children, youth and young families access to mental health, housing, education and employment, and other wrap-around social services.
- Duration : February 2025 to March 2029
- Project stream : Stream 1
- Contribution agreement : $10 million

### Kickstand Network Expansion Project
- Organization : Kickstand
- Description : Kickstand will expand its network with the addition of 5 new Kickstand hubs and offer in-person and virtual sessions across all existing and new sites. It will also implement a provincial peer support program particularly aimed at at-risk youth.
- Duration : August 2025 to March 2029
- Project stream : Stream 1
- Contribution agreement : $10 million

### MindFit Toolkit Youth
- Organization : Canadian Men’s Health Foundation
- Description : Deliver digital mental health supports for male-identifying youth aged 12 to 29, including trans, Two-Spirit, non-binary, and gender non-conforming individuals, by providing tools and resources that connect youth to services in urban and rural areas across Canada.
- Duration : February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.2 million

### Strengthening Connections: Enhancing Mental Health Service Access for Youth Experiencing or at Risk of Homelessness
- Organization: Covenant House Vancouver
- Location: Vancouver, British Columbia
- Description: Improve mental health outcomes for youth, including Indigenous youth, at risk of or experiencing homelessness in Vancouver by enhancing access to and navigation of services.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $2 million

### Enhancing Youth Mental Health Support Through Outreach and Collaboration
- Organization: Kaslo Community Services Society
- Location: Kaslo, British Columbia
- Description: Improve access to trauma-informed and culturally responsive mental health supports for underserved rural youth and families through the addition of a youth outreach worker. Strengthen coordination with schools, primary care, and regional services.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $400,000

### Empowering Youth: Expanding Access and Building Capacity in Cowichan
- Organization: Cowichan Valley Youth Services Society
- Location: Cowichan Valley (Duncan area), British Columbia
- Description: Expand access to quality counselling services for youth in rural, remote and underserved communities in Cowichan Valley through additional clinical expertise.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1 million

### Bridge to Hope: Building Capacity for Youth Mental Health Services
- Organization: BridgePoint Center Inc.
- Location: Milden, Saskatchewan
- Description: Improve access to care for youth affected by eating disorders care by strengthening services, expanding virtual care and providing training to other community organizations to support early identification and intervention.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.5 million

### Youth Crisis Intervention Program
- Organization: Lloydminster Sexual Assault Services
- Location: Lloydminster, Saskatchewan
- Description: Support youth affected by sexual violence by strengthening crisis intervention and trauma-informed supports, enhancing access to specialized services, and enhancing referral pathways. Promote awareness and reduce stigma through public education activities.
- Duration: March 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $800,000

### Promoting Access to Therapy and Healing (PATH)
- Organization: Resilia Community Wellness Centre
- Location: Winnipeg, Manitoba
- Description: Provide culturally safe mental health services and supports for newcomer and immigrant youth and families through counselling, peer support, and mental health literacy workshops.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.8 million

### Building Resilience and Attachment in Youth with Suicidal Ideation in French: A Family Approach
- Organization: Valoris for Children and Adults of Prescott-Russell
- Location: Rockland, Ontario
- Description: Deliver a mental health group program for marginalized youth in Prescott-Russell, including French-speaking families, to reduce wait times and support adolescents experiencing suicidal ideation.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $900,000

### The Help in Hand Program: Leveraging Digital Mental Health Technologies to Support Youth and Schools in Northwestern Ontario
- Organization: Lakehead University
- Location: Thunder Bay, Ontario
- Description: Train staff in school and community organizations on youth mental health, how to use a vetted digital mental health tool that helps youth build coping skills and manage their mental health, and connect youth to appropriate services.
- Duration: March 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: $2 million

### Mobile Youth Walk-In
- Organization: Dufferin Child and Family Services
- Location: Orangeville, Ontario
- Description: Improve access to mental health services for youth aged 12 to 25 in rural Dufferin County, through a fully equipped mobile walk-in unit with trained professionals to reach underserved youth.
- Duration: March 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: $1.5 million

### Transitional Aged Youth (TAY) Initiative
- Organization: Canadian Mental Health Association: Peel Branch
- Location: Brampton, Ontario
- Description: Support youth transitioning from child to adult mental health systems through dedicated staff that will provide coordinated, person-centered care and flexible treatment options.
- Duration: March 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $2.0 million

### Culturally Responsive Mental Health Pathways for Asian Youth in the Greater Toronto Area
- Organization: HF Care
- Location: Toronto, Ontario
- Description: Improve access to culturally appropriate mental health services for Asian youth and families through counselling and connections to services across the Greater Toronto Area and online.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.9 million

### Fostering Mental Health & Wellness in Youth Survivors of Sexual Violence
- Organization: Women’s Support Network of York Region
- Location: Newmarket, Ontario
- Description: Deliver trauma-informed mental health supports for youth survivors of sexual violence, including young women and girls, as well as transgender and gender-diverse youth by expanding access to counselling, crisis services, and caregiver supports.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.8 million

### Scaling KHP’s Online Messaging Service for Youth
- Organization: Kids Help Phone
- Description: Expand the availability of a widget that enables free, one-on-one bilingual access to a crisis texting response service from a community organization’s website or platform, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week across Canada.
- Duration: March 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: $2 million

### Improving access to mental health care in postsecondary education through Francophone community collaboration
- Organization: Université de Moncton
- Location: Moncton, New Brunswick
- Description: Strengthen mental health services for Francophone postsecondary students by improving access to walk-in services, and continuity of care.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.9 million

### Building Resilience and Breaking Barriers: Expanding Accessible Mental Health Services for Youth in Southeastern New Brunswick
- Organization: Atlantic Wellness Community Centre
- Location: Moncton, New Brunswick
- Description: Expand youth mental health services in southeastern New Brunswick by increasing mental health human resources to deliver early supports for underserved youth, including rural, Francophone, newcomers and Indigenous youth.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.3 million

### Dalhousie In Community, With Community: Empowering Youth Mental Health Through Knowledge Sharing and Education
- Organization: Dalhousie University
- Location: Halifax, Nova Scotia
- Description: Strengthen mental health services by providing training and education to community-based organizations to jointly deliver mental health care for youth who face systemic barriers to access, including those in rural communities.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.7 million

### Stronger Together: Empowering 2SLGBTQIA+ Youth and Families
- Organization: PEERS Alliance Inc.
- Location: Charlottetown, Prince Edward Island
- Description: Improve access to culturally appropriate mental health services for 2SLGBTQIA+ youth by expanding programming and supports across urban and rural Prince Edward Island.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.6 million

### YouthConnect: Newcomer Peer Mental Health Program
- Organization: Association for New Canadians
- Location: St. John’s, Newfoundland
- Description: Train peer support workers to deliver mental health supports and education to newcomer youth.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2029
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $1.6 million

### Designing and Implementing National Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Treatment of Youth with Eating Disorders
- Organization: The Governors of The University of Calgary
- Description: Develop and implement updated national guidelines to improve the screening and treatment of eating disorders in children, adolescents and young adults across diverse and underserved communities.
- Duration: November 2025 to August 2027
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $500,000

### Development of Canadian, Youth Focused, Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Early Identification, Screening, Assessment, and Treatment of Problematic Gambling
- Organization: Greo Evidence Insights
- Description: Develop Canada’s first evidence-based clinical guidelines to help identify and treat problem gambling among youth and young adults.
- Duration: February 2026 to March 2028
- Project stream: Stream 2
- Contribution agreement: Approximately $800,000

### Developing a Hospital-Based Consultation-Liaison Service and Collaborative Care Model for Black Youth in Ontario
- Organization : The Centre for Addiction and Mental Health
- Description : Address systemic inequities and improve equitable, timely access to culturally-responsive mental health and substance use care by establishing a Consultation-Liaison Service and Collaborative Care Model for Black youth aged 12 to 29 in Ontario.
- Duration : February 2025 to March 2029
- Project stream : Stream 2
- Contribution agreement : Approximately $4.4 million
- Organization : The Centre for Resilience and Social Development
- Location : Ottawa, Ontario
- Description : Provide and enhance access to culturally-responsive and trauma-informed mental health care to youth and their families in low-equity neighborhoods in Ottawa, including for Black, immigrant, refugee, and racialized youth aged 12 to 24.
- Duration : February 2025 to March 2029
- Project stream : Stream 2
- Contribution agreement : Approximately $2 million

- Get Help Here: Mental health support
